assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files contain: Repository (e.g., 'trustify-backend'), Target Branch ('main'), Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria (with checkbox items), and Test Requirements (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1 through task-5.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: There are exactly 5 task files: task-1-add-advisory-severity-summary-model.md, task-2-add-severity-aggregation-service-method.md, task-3-add-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-add-cache-invalidation-for-advisory-ingestion.md, task-5-add-integration-tests-for-advisory-summary-endpoint.md. 5 is within [3, 10].
All 5 task files have '## Repository' followed by 'trustify-backend': task-1 line 3, task-2 line 3, task-3 line 3, task-4 line 3, task-5 line 3.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency chain: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1. Task 3 depends on Task 2. Task 4 depends on Task 3. Task 5 depends on Task 3. This forms a valid DAG: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> {4, 5}. No cycles exist.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: Creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing model/ directory), Modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (exists in structure). Task 2: Modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (exists). Task 3: Creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing endpoints/ directory), Modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (exists). Task 4: Modifies modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (exists). Task 5: Creates tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing tests/api/ directory), Modifies tests/Cargo.toml (exists). All paths align with the repo-backend.md directory tree.

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map in impact-map.md lists 8 specific changes including: 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary response model struct with fields: critical, high, medium, low, total', 'Add service method to SbomService that queries the sbom_advisory join table, joins to advisory to get severity, deduplicates by advisory ID', 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint handler', 'Add cache invalidation hook in advisory ingestion pipeline'. Each change names specific structs, methods, file paths, and technical details.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks have exactly one repository listed: 'trustify-backend'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive text), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in each task file.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Files to Modify paths all exist in repo-backend.md structure: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs, tests/Cargo.toml. Files to Create paths are new files in existing directories from the structure: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (model/ dir exists), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (endpoints/ dir exists), tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (tests/api/ dir exists). No invented directory structures.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs (SbomSummary)' and 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/details.rs (SbomDetails)'. Task 2 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs', 'entity/sbom_advisory.rs', 'entity/advisory.rs', 'common/src/error.rs'. Task 3 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs', 'common/src/error.rs'. Task 4 references 'modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs', mentions 'sbom_advisory join table'. Task 5 references 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs', 'assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK)' pattern. All tasks cite specific file paths and code patterns from the repository.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks have '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1 line 7-8, task-2 line 7-8, task-3 line 7-8, task-4 line 7-8, task-5 line 7-8.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: No output files contain any reference to 'sha256', 'digest', or '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest'. Searched all files in /tmp/plan-feature-eval-baseline/eval-1/outputs/ with grep and found no matches. There are no comment files or log files showing digest comments were posted.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No task file contains any reference to 'CONVENTIONS.md', 'Applies:', or convention applicability rationales. Grep for 'convention', 'CONVENTIONS', and 'Applies:' across all output files found only incidental uses of the word 'conventions' in lowercase referring to Rust/code conventions (e.g., 'Rust's unsigned integer conventions'), not CONVENTIONS.md-based enrichment. No convention-aware enrichment was performed.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture (feature-standard.md) shows Priority: Major. However, no task file contains 'priority', 'additional_fields', or 'Major'. Grep for 'priority', 'fixVersion', and 'additional_fields' returned no matches in any output file. The priority was not propagated to tasks.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture shows 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0' (non-empty). The CLAUDE.md has no '### Jira Field Defaults' section, so fixVersion scope defaults to 'both', meaning tasks should inherit fixVersions. However, no task file contains 'fixVersions', 'RHTPA', 'additional_fields', or any version reference. The fixVersions were not propagated to tasks.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: No output file contains a summary comment referencing priority ('Major') or fixVersion ('RHTPA 1.5.0'). There is no summary comment file in the outputs directory. The impact-map.md does not mention priority or fixVersion propagation. No evidence of a Step 6c summary comment exists in the outputs.
